$comment: Neutral shipped <errno.h> (POSIX) — c14a (linux) + c15b (macOS, via the c15a per-target const/macro/typedef variant mechanism). THREE surfaces, now PER-FORMAT: (1) the `errno` lvalue object-MACRO selects its accessor by format — `(*__errno_location())` on elf/glibc, `(*__error())` on macho/Darwin; (2) each accessor carries a per-symbol `availableObjectFormats` (__errno_location=elf-only, __error=macho-only) so ONLY the active format's accessor is injected + imported — DSS imports EVERY declared shipped extern, so declaring the wrong-format accessor would plant an undefined import (glibc has no __error; libSystem has no __errno_location); the injected one resolves from the active format's `library` (libc.so.6 elf / libSystem macho). (D-SHIPPED-SYMBOL-PER-TARGET-AVAILABILITY); (3) the E* numbers: the low block (EPERM..ERANGE) AGREES between Linux and Darwin so it stays flat, but the divergent ones carry per-format `variants` (Linux asm-generic vs Darwin sys/errno.h — e.g. EAGAIN 11 vs 35, ETIMEDOUT 110 vs 60, ENOTSUP 95 vs 45, EOPNOTSUPP 95 vs 102, EOVERFLOW 75 vs 84, ENOLCK 37 vs 77, EDEADLK 35 vs 11). c106 pe axis: msvcrt `_errno` accessor (dumpbin-verified export) + `(*_errno())` macro variant + pe arms on the divergent E* (ucrt 10.0.26100.0-verified: EAGAIN 11 [agrees with Linux], ENOLCK 39, EOVERFLOW 132, ENOTSUP 129, EOPNOTSUPP 130, ETIMEDOUT 138, EDEADLK 36; the flat low block EPERM..ERANGE agrees on ALL THREE platforms — ENOMEM 12 joins it, agreeing elf/macho/pe). test_syscall.c (sqlite) drove the +ENOMEM/+EDEADLK need. Gated availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho,pe] (the Linux WSL run target + the macos-latest CI leg, which runs Mach-O natively). The VALUES are interop-critical — verified against the Linux errno-base.h/errno.h + the Darwin xnu sys/errno.h.
